title: biscutella ciliata subsp. applanata
intro: Biscutella ciliata subsp. applanata, also known as a subspecies of the broom cudweed, is a flowering plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Biscutella ciliata subsp. applanata is also valued for its health benefits, which include antioxidant, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate respiratory issues and support overall well-being.
